content.thinking:
The bug is clear: in `src/utils.ts`, the `processItems` function uses `i < items.length - 1` as the loop condition, which stops one iteration early. It should be `i < items.length`.

Let me fix this.

content.text: The bug is clear: the loop condition `i < items.length - 1` stops one index short, skipping the last item. The fix is to change it to `i < items.length`.

content.content: The file /private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775304823583-4j4bhp/src/utils.ts has been updated successfully.

lines: * Process items by converting each to uppercase.
- * Known issue: users report the last item is never processed.

content.text:
All 6 tests pass. Here's a summary of what was fixed:

**Bug:** Off-by-one error in the `for` loop condition in `processItems` (`src/utils.ts`, line 7).

| | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| Loop condition | `i < items.length - 1` | `i < items.length` |

The `- 1` caused the loop to stop one iteration early, so the last element of the array was never pushed into `results`. Removing it makes the loop iterate over all indices (0 through `length - 1`), processing every item including the last one.

result:
All 6 tests pass. Here's a summary of what was fixed:

**Bug:** Off-by-one error in the `for` loop condition in `processItems` (`src/utils.ts`, line 7).

| | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| Loop condition | `i < items.length - 1` | `i < items.length` |

The `- 1` caused the loop to stop one iteration early, so the last element of the array was never pushed into `results`. Removing it makes the loop iterate over all indices (0 through `length - 1`), processing every item including the last one.
